source.quote: Servers that support completions MUST declare the `completions` capability:
Implementations MUST: Validate all completion inputs; Implement appropriate rate limiting; Control access to sensitive suggestions; Prevent completion-based information disclosure

requirements.exclusion: Input validation, rate limiting, and access control are implementation-internal enforcement without a deterministic wire signature.

source.quote: Servers SHOULD return standard JSON-RPC errors for common failure cases: Method not found: `-32601` (Capability not supported); Invalid prompt name: `-32602` (Invalid params); Missing required arguments: `-32602` (Invalid params); Internal errors: `-32603` (Internal error)

requirements.exclusion: Whether a failure case actually occurred is server-side ground truth a trace does not carry.

source.quote: Servers SHOULD: Return suggestions sorted by relevance; Implement fuzzy matching where appropriate; Rate limit completion requests; Validate all inputs

requirements.exclusion: Relevance ordering, fuzziness, rate policy, and input vetting are semantic internals without trace-level observables.

source.quote: Clients SHOULD: Debounce rapid completion requests; Cache completion results where appropriate; Handle missing or partial results gracefully

requirements.exclusion: Debouncing and caching are client-internal and timing-shaped; checks may not consult time (determinism rule).
